mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in RSAFS (retail sales) indicates strengthening consumer demand, which drives higher corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This signal is always active as it relies on the month-over-month change in retail sales.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward S&P 500 return following positive monthly RSAFS changes is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10) over any 5-year rolling period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A very low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS near zero) implies highly accommodative monetary policy, which reduces discount rates on equity cash flows and stimulates risk appetite, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. This signal is always active as the level is continuously evaluated.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward S&P 500 return during periods of FEDFUNDS < 0.25% is not positive on average (mean return <= 0) over any 3-year window, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above a threshold of 7.0%, it signals economic slack and rising recession risk, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward S&P 500 return following UNRATE crossings above 7.0% is not negative in more than 60% of instances over a 10-year history, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The joint effect of rising retail sales (RSAFS) and declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) creates an amplified positive signal for S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. The interaction captures the combined boost from demand strength and monetary accommodation, which is stronger than the sum of individual effects.
failure_condition: If the interaction term (RSAFS_change * FEDFUNDS_level) in a linear regression explaining 60-day SPX returns has a coefficient not significantly positive (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window, the mechanism is invalidated.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of declining industrial production (INDPRO falling month-over-month), further declines signal deepening industrial recession and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. Conversely, rising INDPRO in an expansionary regime signals positive returns, but this hypothesis focuses on the contractionary regime.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward S&P 500 return following two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO is not negative in at least 55% of occurrences over the available history, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 270, indicating elevated inflation, it prompts tighter monetary policy expectations and erodes real earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward S&P 500 return following CPIAUCSL exceeding 270 is not negative in more than 60% of cases over any 5-year window, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals broad-based economic strength, which drives corporate profits and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The interaction is crucial because both sectors must expand to confirm robustness.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward S&P 500 returns following months with both PAYEMS and INDPRO positive month-over-month are not significantly greater (mean > 0, p < 0.05) than returns following negative or mixed signals over a 10-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 1%), a surge in housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong housing demand and wealth effects, boosting consumer confidence and equity returns over 20 trading days. In a high rate environment, the same surge has a muted or negative effect due to mortgage cost concerns.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward S&P 500 return following periods when FEDFUNDS < 1% and HOUST > 1500 is not positive in at least 55% of instances over history, the hypothesis is rejected.
